countNucleotide.name: Count a Nucleotide
countNucleotide.description: Write a function that counts how many times a specific nucleotide (A, C, G, or T) appears in a DNA strand. Return -1 if the nucleotide or any character in the strand is invalid.

nucleotideEmptyStrand.name: Empty strand
nucleotideEmptyStrand.description: An empty strand should return 0 for any valid nucleotide.

nucleotideSingleMatch.name: Single nucleotide strand
nucleotideSingleMatch.description: A single-character strand matching the target should return 1.

nucleotideRepeated.name: Repeated nucleotide
nucleotideRepeated.description: A strand of repeated nucleotides should count all of them.

nucleotideMixedStrandA.name: Count A in mixed strand
nucleotideMixedStrandA.description: Count a specific nucleotide in a mixed strand.

nucleotideMixedStrandT.name: Count T in mixed strand
nucleotideMixedStrandT.description: Count a different nucleotide in the same strand.

nucleotideInvalidNucleotide.name: Invalid nucleotide
nucleotideInvalidNucleotide.description: An invalid nucleotide character should return -1.

nucleotideInvalidStrand.name: Invalid strand
nucleotideInvalidStrand.description: A strand containing invalid characters should return -1.

checkValid.question: How do I check that the character I'm asked about is valid?
checkValid.answer: Write a small helper that asks 'is this character one of A, C, G, T?'. Use it to validate the nucleotide before doing anything else.

invalidNucleotide.question: What do I do if the nucleotide is invalid?
invalidNucleotide.answer: Return `-1` immediately. The spec uses that value to signal an invalid input.

countOccurrences.question: How do I count occurrences in the strand?
countOccurrences.answer: Loop through each character of the strand. If the character matches the nucleotide you're counting, add 1 to a counter. Return the counter at the end.

validateStrand.question: Should I validate the strand too?
validateStrand.answer: Yes. If any character in the strand isn't a valid nucleotide, return `-1`. Check each character before counting.
